place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

review_text:
Hidden away in the Rifle Range greenery, Temasek Club honestly feels like one of those underrated chill spots in Singapore. The whole place has a peaceful resort vibe with lots of nature surrounding it, making it great for family gatherings, weddings, casual dinners or even just escaping the city for awhile.

The atmosphere is probably the biggest selling point here. Many visitors mention the relaxing ambience, lush views and spacious environment that makes it feel less hectic than most clubs in Singapore. There is also a decent Cantonese restaurant within with very nice menu options.

One thing to note is accessibility. If you are driving, parking is manageable and convenient. But if you rely on public transport, getting there can feel slightly out of the way since the club sits deeper into Rifle Range Road.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: On 6 March 2026, I had the privilege of visiting Temasek Club during a Taiwan–Singapore exchange meeting. The venue is beautiful and well maintained, creating a welcoming and refined atmosphere. The service was professional and attentive, and the food was excellent. It was a very pleasant and memorable experience.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
As a new resident in the North shore District Samudera LRT Station is a boon for commuting to and from the city with a short connection to Punggol MRT from either of it's platforms.
Connected to it by a walkway,on abnd above ground is Northshore Plaza One and conveniences like a 24hr supermarket, GIANT and gym.Decatholon has a very large 2 level outlet and Coffee Bean operates all night.Haidilao recently also opened it's outlet here that serves it's hot pot fares until the wee hours of the morning and closes at 4am.
Catch beautiful sunsets here at Northshore from the end of Northshore Plaza 2, a leisurely extension of Plaza 1 while having local fare at Cantine or Dancing Crab on the ground floor that is at the seafront of the Johor Straits...
This LRT Station lives up to its name Samudera...

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Good connection and nice eating place. Coffee shop should be bigger to cater for more food varieties and has sea view.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Opened on 31 March 2017 as part of the Punggol LRT line west loop operated by SBS Transit, this LRT station is located at Punggol Way near the junction with Northshore Walk. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Whiteshores, White Cove, Northshore Bungalows and Punggol Neighbourhood 4 (Northshore Residences I & II, Northshore Edge and Waterfront I & II @ Northshore) bringing them along to Punggol Park Connector, Punggol Tu Di Gong Temple, Marina Country Club, Punggol Town Centre and Northshore Plaza I & II. The bus services serving Samudera LRT station are Go-Ahead Singapore bus services 84G and 84W which ply between Punggol Bus Interchange and New Punggol Road and Go-Ahead Singapore feeder bus service 384 which plies between Punggol Bus Interchange and Northshore Drive. The station takes its name from its geographical location near the sea, with ‘Samudera’ meaning ‘sea’ in Tamil, which is similarly used in Malay.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I commute at Samudera LRT every weekday. Reach this station before 5pm. Its poorly lit and can i say dark. The stairways.. after tap-out area where the convenient store is.. and along the two exits, A and B. Make it bright pls. Even if it is not yet 7pm, its feels dark. Samudera is located near forested area.. so its eerie and unfriendly.
Provide fans too if possible. Vey stuffy since the station is covered up.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
​If you're looking for the most scenic library in Singapore, this is it. Located on the 3rd floor of VivoCity, library@harbourfront is a masterclass in modern, functional design.
​Why it’s a must-visit for me.  The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a breathtaking, panoramic view of the Sentosa waterfront and the cable cars. Snagging one of the yellow deck chairs facing the water is a total game-changer.
​
Despite being in one of Singapore’s busiest malls, it is remarkably peaceful and quiet. The "coastal theme" with its undulating ceilings and blue-green hues makes the entire space feel like a sanctuary.
​
I love the clear distinction between the Adults/Teens zone and the Children's area. It keeps the reading lounges tranquil while the kids have their own interactive "Tinker Truck" and AR books to explore.

It's modern tech, highly efficient with digital newspaper stations, "Learning Pods" for TED Talks, and an easy Grab-n-Go system.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Visited Library @ HarbourFront — one of the nicest local libraries to relax and enjoy some quiet time with a book.
The view overlooking Sentosa Island adds to the calming ambiance, making it a great spot to unwind.

It’s mostly a self-service library with everything fully automated, from borrowing to returning books.
The setup is modern and efficient, making the whole experience smooth and convenient.
A great place for both readers and those looking for a peaceful retreat.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Really love the library in the middle of a crowded mall. Very happy they have a security gantry to allow people to scan passes to enter. Divided session of young kids and other section is although a hassle but necessary because the children session can be a little noisy. Also love they have the self check out gantry which is cool.

Books selection may not have the widest variety but good enough for town. I find limited seatings among bookshelves area although proper table/seats are scattered all around. I find a lot of people occupied those seats for working, studying or leisure reading. I really don't like the people taking advantage to nap there but the librarian did go around stopping them. The other point that was not pleasant are the seats facing the waterfront. While it is definitely such a good experience reading and facing sentosa, the air was really stuffy and not ventilated. The heat from the glass was intense and the air con wasn't strong enough.

Beautifully furnished library.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Really enjoyed visiting VivoCity 🛍️
The mall is HUGE and has pretty much everything you need. Lots of shopping, food options, cafes, and entertainment all in one place. It’s also super convenient since it connects directly to Sentosa, which makes it a great stop before or after exploring the island 🌴🚆
One of my favorite parts was definitely the terrace area 😍 The outdoor space was so pretty and relaxing, especially with the open view, greenery, and waterfront atmosphere. It felt really nice to just sit there for a while and enjoy the vibe, especially around sunset 🌅✨ Such a nice contrast from the busy indoor mall area.
The mall itself was clean, spacious, and easy to walk around despite how big it is. I also liked that there were plenty of seating areas to rest between shopping and exploring. Overall, VivoCity felt more than just a shopping mall, it was honestly a really enjoyable place to spend a few hours 🤍

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This mega mall that serves the residents in the west side of Singapore,  has upgraded recently with the introduction of more eateries at the basement, when one exits from the mrt station. Singapore is a foodie haven , so it is important to pump in popular brands to draw the hungry working people n tourists.  One can see that every outlet is packed during meal time. So it is very competitive for the shops to come up with promotional set menu to attract customers.  Another local pastime is shopping, so sale events are usually held at the front podium area. The mall houses two floors for those who enjoy shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
So many stores to choose from, this mall is huge!
The Christmas Market was great, it was lit nicely and there were a lot of good Christmas shopping options like baked goods, wrapping paper, Christmas signs and cards.
Everything was very clean but since its a huge mall it does get very very busy.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: ​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

The building interior appears to be quite old, and the space does not seem to be effectively utilised. It feels like it could be better planned, but the SAFRA membership is very affordable.

One good thing is that there is a McDonald’s here. It is convenient for drive-through orders, and the interior of the McDonald’s outlet is quite nice and up to date too.

There is also an EnergyOne gym (need to sign up for membership / per entry) on Level 2, above the swimming pool area, which is convenient for people living nearby. The gym is decent and well-equipped, and it comes with shower facilities, a sauna, and a jacuzzi (located at the Level 1 swimming pool, but accessible only through the gym).

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Overall, the transportation itself was smooth and we had no issues with the ride. However, our experience at the station was quite disappointing due to the attitude of one of the staff members.
We were traveling with a young child and accidentally walked into the station area because we did not notice the signage, which was quite small and easy to miss. Instead of guiding us in a calm and helpful manner, an elderly staff member raised his voice at us in a way that felt unnecessary and unfriendly.
We completely understand that there are rules to follow, and we had no intention of going the wrong way. A polite explanation would have been much more appropriate, especially considering we were with a child.
We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
We visited IMM Building, Singapore’s largest outlet mall, and had a good shopping experience. This place really lives up to what many reviewers say — it has all varieties from high-end brands to low-end shops, making it easier to find what you need under one roof. You can discover everything from fashion and sportswear to electronics, home goods, and plenty of dining options.

Many visitors also love the discounts on big brands (up to around 80% at outlet stores), and the mall is spacious and easy to navigate. There are lots of food and beverage outlets to take a break while shopping.

Crowds can get heavier on weekends, and some items in outlet stores may not be the latest season stock, but overall it’s a great place for bargain hunting and casual shopping.

Overall experience was pleasant — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Hum log IMM Building gaye aur overall shopping experience kaafi achhi rahi. Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of Singapore's most convenient MRT interchange stations. Clean, well-maintained, and easy to navigate with clear signage. The station provides smooth connections between multiple MRT lines, making travel across Singapore fast and convenient.
The facilities are excellent, with lifts, escalators, and barrier-free access for all commuters. Trains are frequent, and the station is generally well organized even during busy hours.
Highly recommended for both daily commuters and visitors exploring Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
date of visit: 2025-11-21

2 charging points spotted at the purple/ north east line and quite dirty, no cleaning done.

the charging point further from the ticket office does not work, no electricity when plugged in the phone charger.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: All people favorite place is outram park and special its hub and metro junction to reach anywhere easily all the location like blue, purple, red, brown line

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Outram Park MRT Station is a major transport hub in Singapore, offering seamless connectivity between the East-West (EWL), North-East (NEL), and Thomson-East Coast Lines (TEL). The station is spacious, clean, and well-maintained, reflecting the efficiency of Singapore’s MRT system.

What stands out is the clear signage and accessibility features, including elevators and escalators, which make it easy for everyone to navigate. It’s also conveniently located near key spots like Singapore General Hospital and Chinatown, making it a great starting point for exploring the city.

During peak hours, it can get crowded, but the trains run frequently, so the wait is minimal. Overall, a fantastic and essential MRT station that enhances commuting across Singapore!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Thank you kindly to the many kind and helpful people at Outram mrt today 13/2/2026. I fainted and two bystanders came to assist me, ensuring I was ok and being looked after by staff before they went on their way. The crowd marshal lady at EW line reacted very quickly and called for help. Abdullah (sorry if I got your name wrong) and another lady staff made sure I recovered well and had safe plans to get medical help, constantly checking on me and offering milo and biscuits. May God bless you all for your kind actions today 🙏

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Station very good location but a bit dirty with empty bottles strewn everywhere. Also many beggars sitting near station selling tissues etc but untidy look. Need to do some enforcement. Embrassment to Singapore government.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
When you are at Tan Quee Lan Street, the stark juxtaposition of modern hi-rise architecture and traditional pre-war shophouses is very clear for all to see.

On one side, many restaurants have sprouted in the row of pre-war shophouses making it a very lively place to be in the evenings and weekends.

On the other side, construction is underway for Guocoland’s Midtown Modern and nearby Midtown Bay. These new buildings are next to Shaw Towers and are looking quite impressive before it’s even completed.

In the middle of these two sides is the Bugis MRT which is very conveniently located that once the construction is completed, you can expect that this MRT Station will bring air of foot traffic to this area for live-work-play.

If you travel along Nicoll Highway, we will see the construction going full steam ahead … even Shaw Towers jumped on the bandwagon and got itself rejuvenated so as not to pale in comparison with the sparkling Midtown buildings when it’s ready.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Located within Compass One shopping mall, the library covers an area of 2,136 square meters, New areas in the library include the Early Literacy Area where families can read and bond together, the Tween Space targeted at children aged 10 to 14, and a dedicated Adults section for quiet reading.  It was officially opened on 30 November 2002, comprising of 2 levels - it is convenient and accessible in the shopping centre.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
One of the old malls that had been revamped.

We were here years ago and after coming back bout 15years later, the place looks really different!

Lets dive in!

Location:
Integrated with the bus interchange and mrt, this mall is considered a hub for residents living in woodlands. There are plenty of entrances and is easily accessible everywhere.

Amenities:
Other than a furniture shop, you can find almost everything here. We were in awe and the food selections that we can find here. Not forgetting the gadget shops that have competitive prices. They also have a prayer room, which we were glad to use.

Parking:
Despite the many accesible options around, this mall has lots of parking lots available., subjected to non-peak hours yeah😅. Alternatively, there are also other nearby areas to park actually.

All in all, if you are up for an exercise to walk, this is one place to head down to. After walking on all floors, treat yourself to shopping and a good meal too!

Highly recommended from us!

Cheers!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Explored Causeway Point in a day. Anchor tenants like Metro Store, Courts for electrical and furniture, Uniqlo Japanese fashion wear. Lots of F&B selections from Kopitiam Bagus halal food court, Food Republic food court, Chinese, Japanese, Korean & western cuisines including lots of fried chicken - Texas, KFC, Jollibee, 4Fingers , Chagee, and lots of BbTea shops. Bakeries & Patisserie like Swee Heng, Annabelle, rrooll. You can spend a whole day exploring beautiful home decor at Madera and kitchen needs. To pamper yourselves with a pair of new glasses or shades from the few optical stores as well as beauty salons for hair and nails. Nostalgic snacks store, automated machines for the SGFR viral snack, Popmart machines can be found too.  From Causeway Point basement it will lead you to Woods Square and Woodlands Civic Center. Highly recommended to visit.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Wow many eateries at the basement offering good food here. Also first floor have a lot of nice shops selling food for takeaway all in one shopping mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Causeway Point is one of the older heartland malls in Singapore. Located just outside Woodlands MRT station, which is now an interchange to TEL line, and a bus interchange, it is in a very strategic location which explains why it’s never not busy. Causeway Point is also connected to Woodlands Civic Center and Woods Square. Beside it is also a location for occasional temporary Pasar Malam.

So, if you don’t like people, this place can be hellish. On the other side, it’s also a very convenient place to get everything you need from grocery, stationery, household items, electronic, pet stuff, food, everything you need.

The mall has everything, from Cinema to Yakun, Popular bookstore to FairPrice supermarket. There are many food varieties to choose from including two food courts. There are Metro department store and Courts. There are pharmacies like Watson, Guardian, and Unity.

Pros: You can find almost everything you need.

Cons: People! So many people!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Love ❤️ Causeway Point.

It’s near my house 🏠 and it has a wide variety of stores under one roof. Satisfy all my needs.

My daughter and I often went there to eat.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:
Bugis Street is one of the best places to visit in Singapore for shopping, street food, and local vibes. The area is always lively and full of energy, with many affordable fashion items, souvenirs, snacks, and accessories to explore. Really suitable for tourists or anyone who enjoys walking around and discovering unique shops.

There are also many food stalls and nearby cafés around Bugis, making it a great place to relax after shopping. The atmosphere becomes even more interesting at night with the bright lights and busy crowd. Convenient location, easy to access by MRT, and a nice place to experience the modern and local side of Singapore at the same time. Highly recommended for shopping lovers and first-time visitors to Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I absolutely love this traditional market. It sells all kinds of goods, modern and traditional items, food and drinks, souvenirs, and various trinkets.

There's also a large fruit stall outside. If you're staying at a nearby hotel and craving fruit, this is definitely an excellent choice, incredible value and extremely fresh.

The food options here are overwhelming. The best way is to eat while you walk, and you can definitely eat until you're full. After you're full, there are still all kinds of desserts and drinks to enjoy.

This place can easily rival traditional markets and night markets from around the world. It's highly recommended for a visit!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The street is always bustling with energy, filled with countless small shops and stalls offering trendy clothes, accessories, souvenirs, and much more at affordable prices. It’s a great place for budget shopping and picking up unique items.
Even if you’re not planning to shop a lot, just walking through the market is an experience in itself. There are also plenty of food stalls and drink options around, so you can enjoy local snacks and refreshing juices while exploring.
It can get quite crowded, especially in the evenings, so be prepared for that. But overall, it’s a must-visit place in Singapore for shopping lovers and anyone who enjoys vibrant street markets!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: We went to check on the souvenir prices and among all souvenir places we’ve been, we think bugis street has the best prices already. Super affordable and not so much crowd. You can haggle a little and have some food and snacks that you can try around while shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Bugis Street is a lively and bustling shopping spot that a lot of visitors recommend for a fun and budget-friendly experience. Many people love how wide and varied the shops are, with everything from affordable fashion and accessories to quirky souvenirs, making it a great place to explore and shop without spending too much.
The street food stalls and snack options add to the vibrant atmosphere — you can easily spend some time tasting local bites while browsing through the stalls. It does get crowded, especially in the evenings and on weekends, but that’s part of the energetic vibe here.
Overall, it’s a great place for casual shopping, good street food, and vibrant street life — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Bugis Street ek bahut lively aur bustling shopping destination hai jise kaafi log budget-friendly shopping ke liye recommend karte hain. Yahan shops ka variety wide hai — saste fashion, accessories, souvenirs aur unique items milte hain, jis se shopping explore karna fun ho jata hai.
Street food stalls aur local snacks bhi yahan ka vibe aur enhance karte hain — shopping ke beech mein local bites try karna easy aur enjoyable hota hai. Thoda crowd hota hai, especially evenings aur weekends par, lekin yeh Bugis Street ka energetic charm hi hai.
Overall, casual shopping, good street food aur vibrant atmosphere ke liye ek badiya place — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Am I the only one feeling a bit hot in here? 🤔 Haven't come to this library and the cafe downstairs for a long time. I prefer here because many available seats. And I am someone who appreciates some little privacy so I can say it is better (than West Mall 😅) although we can't control the noise of some people. Hope they don't chit chat in the library...

Hope they can volume down a bit too. Even the Level 4 kids section was moderate. Convenient too, there is toilet. That's how I found out my menses comes rather sudden and unexpectedly 😭

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Other libary blast the air con which is okay
This libary doesn’t bother to turn it on on the upper floors??? Booked S301 and when I came there was a pile of stuff there, told the librarian but she said cannot do anything.. the person never came back and I had to book another seat which this uncle was using my wall socket: asked staff again tell me don’t be so annoying then I put one of my power bank at the quickcharge station, staff told me cannot charge power banks?? It literally says you can charge power banks?

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Convenient place to study. Relaxing and clean. The staff provided enough tables and chairs. But the only things is very limited socket to charge your laptop. It is better to fully charge your devices before come :)

review_text: Excellent place for self study at level 3. Level have cafe and sofa chair. And also charging station for phone powerbank and ipad etc. There is also workshop for enhancement on IT product. But might be different every month.

This swimmimg complex has 1 kids' water play area and a 50m length pool. Not a big complex but sufficient for the number of users.

There is a big pond when looking out from pool, open space and doesn't feel clamp. There is a haeker center across the street and a LRT station.

review_text: 1st time been there to join SG Digital course.  It's has good environment facing a pond n a swimming pool.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Weekdays is ok. Weekend is a fish market. Screaming infants and kids running around. Badly controlled during weekends due to small place. Avoid Sundays if you need a place to read.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I would like to commend the NEX Library for its attentiveness and highly proactive staff. It’s quite impressive how quickly they notice patrons—within just 30 minutes of me sitting on one of the couches, I was disturbed not once, but three times.

What stood out most was the level of dedication shown. One staff member even went the extra mile by physically slapping my chair to wake me up, followed by a firm reminder of “don’t sleep,” delivered publicly. It’s rare to see such enthusiasm in ensuring that no one gets too comfortable.

While I understand that seating is meant to be shared, I didn’t realise that briefly resting would warrant such persistent interruptions and physical gestures. It does make me wonder about the purpose of having couches in the library if taking a short rest isn’t acceptable.

Perhaps moving forward, the same level of attentiveness could be paired with a bit more courtesy and respect when approaching patrons. After all, maintaining a welcoming environment is just as important as enforcing rules.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really like the Serangoon library because there is a specific section where there are about 15 seats alone (near the entrance) And I think that's very helpful. There also is a children's section for 10-12 year olds and a 6-8 year old section right next to the 10-12 section. They also have comics for people that like to read comics. Close to the entrance. They have this display of books and I've picked a few books from the display that I've liked so that's very nice. There is a fairly large section for Fiction and I am a Fiction fan so that's great for me. There are multiple computers there too. I don't know if it's the best place to study as a student though. But the library's location is really nice because just one level down (after you exit the library) there's a mall. I personally really like the mall because there's a Fair Price Xtra, restaurants, gadget stores and more. I wish there were more isolated seats instead of many at a certain section. The location outside of the library and mall is also nice because there is a bus station and a taxi station. Overall I would say that the Serangoon library is a very nice library to visit.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Visited three times and harassed by staff each time. They have a children’s section but their expectations for toddler behavior are unrealistic. Zero empathy. I sent my family outside just to check out our books in peace. Even then I would still get nagged with unsolicited and irrelevant advice.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall experience is good just that at around 4pm onwards, the library will be super crowded. They have programme room which is free seating without the need to book table but from 1pm onwards, all tables are occupied. Should add another programme room. Also the library can be very cold too
